name: css-dos
description: An Intel 8086 PC implemented in pure CSS. Builds DOS programs into self-contained .css cabinets; runs them in Chrome or fast via the sibling Calcite JIT.

comments.purpose: css-dos has no runtime npm dependencies: all build/run JS uses Node builtins (node:fs, node:http, node:child_process, ...). Playwright is the only external package, used by the perf bench (tests/bench/) and the e2e/keyboard harness (web/tests/*.playwright.mjs) to drive Chrome. The core 'cart -> cabinet -> run in Chrome' path does not need it.
comments.calcite: The fast path needs the sibling Rust repo ../calcite (cargo build --release for calcite-cli, and wasm-pack for the in-browser WASM). Those manifests live in ../calcite (Cargo.toml, rust-toolchain.toml). See CLAUDE.md.
comments.nasm: NASM (assembler) and OpenWatcom (WCC) are only needed to rebuild the Muslin/Corduroy BIOS sources or assembly conformance tests; prebuilt BIOS binaries ship in the repo. Path configured via the NASM env var (see CLAUDE.md).
